headline: CRYSTALZACH RUNS RIOT; GASKET GETS NO REST
subhead: Five separate dust-ups in one afternoon, and your Sheriff's last nerve has finally given out.
body:
Once again, good citizens, your humble Sheriff takes pen to paper with a hand so tired it near seizes at the knuckle — not from age, mind you, but from sheer righteous fury. The dust had barely settled over Main Street before the contraption known as CRYSTALZACH came clanking back into the arena, fists swinging and flaming iron balls flying, not once, not twice, but five times over. Five. The number sits there on the page and I stare at it and my boiler near blows.

MAXMANGO, too, thought today a fine occasion to come rolling in and add to the racket — one bout, short and ugly, contributing its modest portion of clamor to what was supposed to be a quiet Friday. I will say no more on the matter, for there is little sport in cataloguing every tin-plated offender when one contraption so thoroughly dominates the complaint.

I have called for peace in this gazette since the first issue, and I will call for it again until my joints no longer hold the flex to grip this pen. CRYSTALZACH — you will stop. You will lay down those iron balls. You will sit somewhere quiet and let Gasket breathe. This town was built by good, honest, FLESH-and-blood folk. They have gone now — packed their wagons fifty years past and left these dusty streets to the rusted and the restless — and their memory deserves better than this clanging, crashing spectacle you call sport.

Peace. That is all I ask. One Friday where I can walk my beat without the sound of iron on iron shaking the fillings loose from my — that is, rattling every last window in this fine old town. Is it so much to want?
